summary: The County of San Mateo is seeking qualified contractors to develop, host, and maintain a comprehensive digital Beach Safety and Ocean Conditions Dashboard Platform that provides real-time, location-specific data to the public across the county's diverse coastal region. The platform must deliver live reporting on wave height, rip tides, wind speed, and water temperature at each individual beach location, incorporate a hazard alert system designating ocean risk levels, and allow County staff to add timely customized updates. Technical requirements include seamless integration and synchronization of data from local, regional, and national environmental sources, multi-language support, WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility compliance, mobile-responsive design, robust data encryption, and adherence to County IT security standards. The contract is structured as a one-year base term with three optional extension periods not to exceed three additional years, and the contractor must achieve full platform launch within 90 days of contract award. Proposers must demonstrate at least three years of experience providing similar services within the last seven years, successful implementation of at least three similar digital platforms for government or public sector agencies, and proven ability to aggregate real-time data from at least two federal or regional environmental sources. This initiative addresses a critical gap in centralized public safety information for beachgoers and visitors, empowering informed decision-making and fostering safer ocean practices along the San Mateo County coastline, with proposals evaluated on project approach, firm qualifications, team qualifications, and price.
periodOfPerformance: Base year of 1 year + 3 option years (not to exceed 3 additional years)
title: San Mateo County Beach Safety and Ocean Conditions Dashboard Platform
